heading: Federal program to assist establishment and operation of nonprofit, member-run health insurance issuers
label: 18042 Federal program to assist establishment and operation of nonprofit, member-run health insurance issuers

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to carry out the purposes of this section to be known as the Consumer Operated and Oriented Plan (CO–OP) program.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: It is the purpose of the CO–OP program to foster the creation of qualified nonprofit health insurance issuers to offer qualified health plans in the individual and small group markets in the States in which the issuers are licensed to offer such plans.

children.heading: Loans and grants under the CO–OP program
children.label: b Loans and grants under the CO–OP program
A
children.text: loans to provide assistance to such person in meeting its start-up costs; and

children.label: B
children.text: grants to provide assistance to such person in meeting any solvency requirements of States in which the person seeks to be licensed to issue qualified health plans.

children.heading: Requirements for awarding loans and grants
children.label: 2 Requirements for awarding loans and grants
i
children.text: take into account the recommendations of the advisory board established under paragraph (3);

children.label: ii
children.text: give priority to applicants that will offer qualified health plans on a Statewide basis, will utilize integrated care models, and have significant private support; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure that there is sufficient funding to establish at least 1 qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer in each State, except that nothing in this clause shall prohibit the Secretary from funding the establishment of multiple qualified nonprofit health insurance issuers in any State if the funding is sufficient to do so.

children.heading: States without issuers in program
children.label: B States without issuers in program
children.text: If no health insurance issuer applies to be a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer within a State, the Secretary may use amounts appropriated under this section for the awarding of grants to encourage the establishment of a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer within the State or the expansion of a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer from another State to the State.

children.label: I
children.text: any requirement under this section for such person to be treated as a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer; and

children.label: II
children.text: any requirements contained in the agreement for such person to receive such loan or grant.

children.heading: Restrictions on use of Federal funds
children.label: ii Restrictions on use of Federal funds
I
children.text: for carrying on propaganda, or otherwise attempting, to influence legislation; or

children.label: II
children.text: for marketing.

children.label: I
children.text: 110 percent of the aggregate amount of loans and grants received under this section; plus

children.label: II
children.text: interest on the aggregate amount of loans and grants received under this section for the period the loans or grants were outstanding.

children.heading: Time for awarding loans and grants
children.label: D Time for awarding loans and grants
children.text: The Secretary shall not later than , award the loans and grants under the CO–OP program and begin the distribution of amounts awarded under such loans and grants.July 1, 2013

children.heading: Repayment of loans and grants
children.label: 3 Repayment of loans and grants
children.text: Not later than , and prior to awarding loans and grants under the CO–OP program, the Secretary shall promulgate regulations with respect to the repayment of such loans and grants in a manner that is consistent with State solvency regulations and other similar State laws that may apply. In promulgating such regulations, the Secretary shall provide that such loans shall be repaid within 5 years and such grants shall be repaid within 15 years, taking into consideration any appropriate State reserve requirements, solvency regulations, and requisite surplus note arrangements that must be constructed in a State to provide for such repayment prior to awarding such loans and grants.July 1, 2013

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The advisory board under this paragraph shall consist of 15 members appointed by the Comptroller General of the United States from among individuals with qualifications described in .section 1395b–6(c)(2) of this title

children.heading: Standards
children.label: i Standards
children.text: Any individual appointed under subparagraph (A) shall meet ethics and conflict of interest standards protecting against insurance industry involvement and interference.

children.heading: Original appointments
children.label: ii Original appointments
children.text: The original appointment of board members under subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be made no later than 3 months after .March 23, 2010

children.heading: Vacancy
children.label: C Vacancy
children.text: Any vacancy on the advisory board shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: No compensation for members of advisory board
children.label: i No compensation for members of advisory board
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), a member of the advisory board may not receive pay, allowances, or benefits by reason of their service on the board.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: ii Travel expenses
children.text: Each member shall receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5.

children.heading: Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: E Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall apply to the advisory board, except that shall not apply.section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: Termination
children.label: F Termination
children.text: The advisory board shall terminate on the earlier of the date that it completes its duties under this section or .December 31, 2015

children.heading: Qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer
children.label: c Qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer
A
children.text: that is organized under State law as a nonprofit, member corporation;

children.label: B
children.text: substantially all of the activities of which consist of the issuance of qualified health plans in the individual and small group markets in each State in which it is licensed to issue such plans; and

children.label: C
children.text: that meets the other requirements of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the organization or a related entity (or any predecessor of either) was a health insurance issuer on ; orJuly 16, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: the organization is sponsored by a State or local government, any political subdivision thereof, or any instrumentality of such government or political subdivision.

children.label: A
children.text: the governance of the organization is subject to a majority vote of its members;

children.label: B
children.text: its governing documents incorporate ethics and conflict of interest standards protecting against insurance industry involvement and interference; and

children.label: C
children.text: as provided in regulations promulgated by the Secretary, the organization is required to operate with a strong consumer focus, including timeliness, responsiveness, and accountability to members.

children.heading: Profits inure to benefit of members
children.label: 4 Profits inure to benefit of members
children.text: An organization shall not be treated as a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer unless any profits made by the organization are required to be used to lower premiums, to improve benefits, or for other programs intended to improve the quality of health care delivered to its members.

children.heading: Compliance with State insurance laws
children.label: 5 Compliance with State insurance laws
children.text: An organization shall not be treated as a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer unless the organization meets all the requirements that other issuers of qualified health plans are required to meet in any State where the issuer offers a qualified health plan, including solvency and licensure requirements, rules on payments to providers, and compliance with network adequacy rules, rate and form filing rules, any applicable State premium assessments and any other State law described in .section 18044(b) of this title

children.heading: Coordination with State insurance reforms
children.label: 6 Coordination with State insurance reforms
children.text: An organization shall not be treated as a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer unless the organization does not offer a health plan in a State until that State has in effect (or the Secretary has implemented for the State) the market reforms required by part A of title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.] (as amended by subtitles A and C of this Act).42 U.S.C. 300gg

children.heading: Establishment of private purchasing council
children.label: d Establishment of private purchasing council

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Qualified nonprofit health insurance issuers participating in the CO–OP program under this section may establish a private purchasing council to enter into collective purchasing arrangements for items and services that increase administrative and other cost efficiencies, including claims administration, administrative services, health information technology, and actuarial services.

children.heading: Council may not set payment rates
children.label: 2 Council may not set payment rates
children.text: The private purchasing council established under paragraph (1) shall not set payment rates for health care facilities or providers participating in health insurance coverage provided by qualified nonprofit health insurance issuers.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the application of the antitrust laws to any private purchasing council (whether or not established under this subsection) or to any qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer participating in such a council.

children.heading: Antitrust laws
children.label: B Antitrust laws
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “antitrust laws” has the meaning given the term in subsection (a) of . Such term also includes to the extent that such section 45 applies to unfair methods of competition.section 12 of title 15section 45 of title 15

children.heading: Limitation on participation
children.label: e Limitation on participation
children.text: No representative of any Federal, State, or local government (or of any political subdivision or instrumentality thereof), and no representative of a person described in subsection (c)(2)(A), may serve on the board of directors of a qualified nonprofit health insurance issuer or with a private purchasing council established under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: participate in any negotiations between 1 or more qualified nonprofit health insurance issuers (or a private purchasing council established under subsection (d)) and any health care facilities or providers, including any drug manufacturer, pharmacy, or hospital; and

children.label: B
children.text: establish or maintain a price structure for reimbursement of any health benefits covered by such issuers.

children.heading: Competition
children.label: 2 Competition
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as authorizing the Secretary to interfere with the competitive nature of providing health benefits through qualified nonprofit health insurance issuers.

children.heading: Appropriations
children.label: g Appropriations
children.text: There are hereby appropriated, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $6,000,000,000 to carry out this section.

children.heading: Study
children.label: 1 Study
children.text: The Comptroller General of the General Accountability Office shall conduct an ongoing study on competition and market concentration in the health insurance market in the United States after the implementation of the reforms in such market under the provisions of, and the amendments made by, this Act. Such study shall include an analysis of new issuers of health insurance in such market.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: The Comptroller General shall, not later than December 31 of each even-numbered year (beginning with 2014), report to the appropriate committees of the Congress the results of the study conducted under paragraph (1), including any recommendations for administrative or legislative changes the Comptroller General determines necessary or appropriate to increase competition in the health insurance market.
